note: Shared Python/TS classifier fixtures. Both sides MUST return identical outcome (+ winningLane/verbMatch where given). Run against BOTH claude/hooks/sidecoach_lanes.py (test_classifier_parity.py) AND sidecoach/mcp-server/src/keyword-resolver.ts (classifier-parity.test.ts). NUDGE_ELIGIBLE cases set eligible=true. The hook-only cooldown -> NUDGE/SILENT mapping is NOT covered here (see test-sidecoach-keyword.sh).
parityNotes: ABBREVIATION MASKING IS NOT A DIVERGENCE: Python segment_clauses masks abbreviation periods (e.g. / i.e. / etc.) with \x00; the TS segmentClauses masks them with a space. Both are length-preserving and proven span-identical across this corpus and the unit suites. Do NOT 'fix' one side to match the other assuming a bug.
Expected values are the ACTUAL verified behavior of both classifiers in P1, not aspirational. Lexicon weight calibration is out of P1 scope (spec section 3).

Adversarial cases included so any Python/TS divergence is caught: prefix-trap (', butter' must not split), cross-sentence OUT_OF_SCOPE, bare-vs-qualified UI noun, grouped MAX-not-sum, route_margin==2 ROUTE, and a quoted-verb-suppressed SILENT.

cases.prompt: The landing page is done. Make the migration production-ready.
Don't make the API production-ready; make the landing page production-ready.
audit this and make it production-ready
the dashboard should feel more fun
I have a TypeScript interface I need to refactor
fix the packet header parsing in the network layer
tone the hero down, it's too busy
keep iterating on the dashboard until it passes
make it production-ready, butter the migration aside
the dashboard must be production-ready and ship-ready
build me a dashboard from scratch and make it production-ready
